info.title: robotsix-central-deploy Lifecycle API
info.description: Start, stop, restart, and inspect suite services.

get.description:
Return current system settings. Secrets are returned as ``"***"``
when set, or ``""`` when empty.

Reads from the *effective* config (env-var values overlaid by any
stored settings) so that env-var credentials are visible in the UI
even before the operator has saved settings via the UI.

put.description:
Update system settings, persist to disk, and hot-apply where possible.

Only fields explicitly present in the request body are changed
(PATCH semantics); unmentioned fields keep their current stored values.
To explicitly clear a field, send its zero value (e.g. ``""`` or ``0``).

- Secret fields sent as ``"***"`` preserve the existing stored value.
- ``log_level`` is validated via Pydantic; an invalid value returns 422.
- All settings *except* ``registry_check_interval`` take effect immediately
  without restart.

# NOTE: ``registry_check_interval`` changes are persisted and reflected
  in ``app.state.config``, but the background ``_registry_check_loop``
  captures its interval at startup via a positional argument.  Updating
  the config alone does **not** alter the running task's sleep period.
  A full service restart is required for an interval change to take
  effect on the background loop.

# NOTE: Volume-audit settings (``volume_audit_enabled``,
  ``volume_audit_interval_seconds``, ``volume_audit_growth_threshold_pct``,
  ``volume_audit_min_delta_bytes``) are persisted and reflected in
  ``app.state.config``, but the ``VolumeAuditScheduler.loop()`` captures
  its interval at startup via a positional argument.  A full service
  restart is required for interval/threshold changes to take effect on
  the running background task.

# NOTE: Backend-consumed settings (e.g. ``docker_socket_url``,
  ``docker_sdk_timeout``) are captured by
  ``DockerSdkBackend`` at construction time — the backend is now built
  *after* the ``system_settings.json`` overlay at startup, so a full
  service restart applies these settings.  Updating the config at
  runtime alone does **not** alter the running backend.

get.description: Host disk usage and Docker storage breakdown.

post.description:
Prune Docker build cache and dangling images, return bytes freed.

Dangling images that are rollback targets (deployed or previous digests
recorded in the service store) are protected from removal.

get.description:
Compare the running server image digest against the registry.

``supported`` is false when the server is not containerised (or the
execution backend cannot tell); ``update_available`` is only true when
both the running and the latest digest resolved and differ.

post.description:
Launch the one-shot updater; the server restarts shortly after.

Returns 202 immediately — the dashboard polls ``/health`` /
``GET /system/update`` until the recreated server answers with the new
digest. 503 when self-update is unsupported, 502 when the updater
container fails to launch.

get.description: Current volume audit state (sizes and growth). Returns enabled=false when subsystem is off.

get.summary: List Docker volumes owned by no component and not in use
get.description:
Return the prune-safe orphan volumes and their total size.

Orphans are Docker volumes declared by no registered component and not
attached to any container — leftovers from removed/re-onboarded components.

post.summary: Remove orphan Docker volumes (owned by no component, not in use)
post.description:
Delete orphan volumes (IRREVERSIBLE).

The eligible set is recomputed server-side on every call — a component's
own volumes and in-use volumes are never touched even if their names are
passed in ``names``.  With no ``names`` (or ``names=null``) every orphan is
pruned; with an explicit list, only names that are still genuine orphans
are removed and the rest are reported under ``skipped``.

get.summary: List files in a data volume
get.description:
Return immediate children of a directory within a named volume.

Only volumes declared in at least one component's ``named_volumes`` are
browsable.  ``path`` defaults to the volume root.

get.summary: Read a text file from a data volume
get.description:
Return the text content of a file within a named volume.

Files larger than ``VOLUME_CAT_MAX_BYTES`` are truncated (``truncated=True``).
Binary files (NUL byte or non-UTF-8) return ``binary=True`` and ``content=null``.
Return all managed services with their current state and optional config metadata.

get.summary: List registered components for config-form URL suggestions
get.description:
Return every registered component's id, container_name, and first
container port so the dashboard config form can offer one-click URL
suggestions for ``*_url`` / ``*_base_url`` fields.

Return full status for a service: live state, health, image digests,
update availability, and sibling health.

Raises 404 if the service is not found. Persists refreshed state and
digest data to the store.

delete.summary: Remove an onboarded component and optionally its container
delete.description:
Remove an onboarded component and optionally its container and volumes.

Deletes the service record, env/secrets, config.json, and component config.
Optionally stops and removes the Docker container (``stop_container``) and
deletes data volumes (``remove_volumes``, irreversible).  Idempotent —
succeeds even when some persisted state is already absent (e.g. the
component config was cleared).  Raises 404 only when *neither* a service
record nor a component config exists for *name*.

parameters.name: stop_container

schema.description: Stop and remove the managed container (true) or leave it running (false)
schema.title: Stop Container

parameters.description: Stop and remove the managed container (true) or leave it running (false)

parameters.name: remove_volumes

schema.description: Also delete the component's data volumes (IRREVERSIBLE — destroys stored data)
schema.title: Remove Volumes

parameters.description: Also delete the component's data volumes (IRREVERSIBLE — destroys stored data)

get.description:
Return the current health status string for a service.

Raises 404 if the service is not found.

Stream container log output as a plain-text response.

Supports optional tail, since, and follow query parameters.
Raises 404 if the service is not found.
Raises 422 if tail is out of range (1-10000).

parameters.name: follow

schema.description: If true, stream new log lines as they arrive
schema.title: Follow

parameters.description: If true, stream new log lines as they arrive

post.description:
Start a service. Idempotent — returns success if already running or starting.

Transitions the service through STARTING to RUNNING (or FAILED on error).
Raises 404 on missing service, 409 if the current state does not allow a
start, and 500 on backend failure. Sibling services are started on a
best-effort basis.

Stop a service. Idempotent — returns success if already stopped or stopping.

Transitions the service through STOPPING to STOPPED (or FAILED on error).
Raises 404 on missing service, 409 if the current state does not allow a
stop, and 500 on backend failure. Sibling services are stopped on a
best-effort basis.

Restart a service. Idempotent — returns success if a restart is already in progress.

Transitions the service through RESTARTING to RUNNING (or FAILED on error).
Raises 404 on missing service, 409 if the current state does not allow a
restart, and 500 on backend failure. Sibling services are restarted on a
best-effort basis.

post.summary: Refetch deploy/docker-compose.yml from the repo and update stored contract
post.description:
Re-parse the component's deploy/docker-compose.yml and update stored settings.

Contract-derived fields (image, ports, mounts, command, entrypoint,
health check, siblings, labels, etc.) are refreshed from the repo HEAD.
Operator-set fields (repo_id, caretaker_auto_update, mem_limit) and
environment overrides in the EnvStore are left untouched.  The endpoint
returns which fields changed so the operator can decide whether a
redeploy is needed.

404.description: Component not found or repo has no deploy/docker-compose.yml

422.description: Repo fetch failed or compose parse failed

post.summary: Deploy a new image version for a service (async)
post.description:
Queue a deploy for a service and return immediately with a job id.

Optionally accepts a specific image reference; defaults to the component's
configured image.  The deploy runs as a background job — the caller polls
``GET /services/deploy-jobs/{job_id}`` for progress.

Returns 202 with a ``job_id`` so the UI can poll progress.  Raises 404 if
the service or component config is not found, 409 when a deploy is already
in progress for the component and no job record exists (e.g. caretaker
deploy), and 503 when the registry checker is not loaded.

404.description: Service or component config not found

409.description: Deploy already in progress for this component

get.summary: Poll the status of a background deploy job
get.description: Return the current phase of a background deploy job.

post.summary: Rollback a service to a prior image digest
post.description:
Roll back a service to a previously recorded image digest.

When *digest* is absent/None: swaps the deployed and previous digests
(the original one-step rollback). When *digest* is present: validates it
appears in the component's deploy history, deploys that digest via the
backend, and records the rollback.

Raises 404 if the service or component config is not found, 409 if no
prior digest or the target digest is not in history, and 500 on backend
failure. Sibling services are rolled back on a best-effort basis
(one-step behaviour only).

get.summary: Get deploy history for a service
get.description:
Return the deploy history for a service, most-recent-first.

Returns 200 with an empty ``entries`` list when no history is recorded.
Raises 404 if the service is not found.

get.summary: Get config.json schema and current values for a service
get.description:
Return the config.json schema and current masked values for a service.

Raises 404 if the service has no config schema.

put.summary: Merge and save config.json values for a service
put.description:
Merge and save config.json values for a service, then write to the config volume.

Restarts the running container (and any siblings sharing the config
volume) so new values take effect immediately. Returns 204 No Content.
Raises 404 if the service has no config schema.

post.summary: Import live volume content into the config store, clearing drift
post.description:
Read the live volume file and store it as the new *current*, clearing drift.

The imported dict is stored as-is (real secret values preserved, since the
volume holds real values). The volume hash is updated to match, so subsequent
drift checks see a clean state.

404.description: Service has no config schema or config volume

post.summary: Refetch config/config.schema.json from the repo and replace the stored template
post.description:
Replace the stored config template with the repo's committed schema.

Components onboarded before the schema-driven config keep the legacy raw
template captured at onboard time; this refetches ``config/config.schema.json``
from the repo HEAD so the typed schema (field types, enums, descriptions)
reaches the dashboard without re-onboarding. Stored *values* are untouched.

404.description: Component not found or repo has no config/config.schema.json

422.description: Repo fetch failed or schema is invalid JSON

post.summary: Run a repo-declared config-assist command in a one-shot container and return auto-filled config
post.description:
Run a repo-declared config-assist command in a one-shot container.

Fetches fresh config-assist metadata from the component's git repo, runs
the detect/assist command inside a temporary container with the config
volume mounted, and merges detected fields into the user-submitted values.
Raises 400 if no config-assist command or config volume is configured,
404 if the component is not found, and 504 if the assist command times out.

400.description: No config-assist command or config volume configured

get.summary: Get stored environment variables and secret keys for a service
get.description:
Return stored environment variables and masked secret keys for a service.

Secret values are never exposed — only the key names are returned, each
masked as ``"***"``. Raises 404 if the service is not found.

put.summary: Upsert environment variables and secrets for a service
put.description:
Create or update environment variables and secrets for a service.

Optionally update the memory limit for the component's container.
Returns 204 No Content on success. Raises 404 if the service is not found.

post.summary: Add env keys newly declared by the repo's compose contract
post.description:
Re-read the repo's compose env contract and seed newly declared keys.

The env key set belongs to the repo's contract, not the operator: keys
with a default value are added as plain env entries, keys declared empty
are added as secret slots — mirroring onboard seeding. Existing values
are never modified, and keys the contract no longer declares are only
reported, not deleted.

422.description: Repo fetch or compose parse failed

delete.summary: Delete an environment variable or secret key for a service
delete.description:
Delete a single environment-variable or secret key for a service.

Returns 204 No Content on success. Raises 404 if the service or key
is not found.

get.description: Return the current caretaker status.

schema.title: Response Get Caretaker Status Caretaker Status Get

post.description:
Fetch and parse a service repo's docker-compose.yml, returning a DerivedSpec.

The caller reviews the spec before confirming onboarding via `/onboard/confirm`.

Persist a reviewed DerivedSpec, then schedule the deploy as a background job.

Returns 202 with a job id so the caller can poll ``GET /onboard/jobs/{job_id}``
for progress.

get.description: Return the current phase of an onboard background deploy job.
Return the current Claude authentication status.

post.description:
Generate a PKCE challenge and return the OAuth authorization URL.

The operator visits the URL, authorizes, and pastes the code shown on
Anthropic's callback page into ``POST /claude-auth/login/complete``.

Exchange the pasted authorization code for tokens and persist them.

On success ``.credentials.json`` is written into the ``claude-auth``
volume with ownership ``1000:1000`` and mode ``0600``.
Discard an in-progress PKCE login session.

Write credentials JSON directly into the ``claude-auth`` volume.

The JSON is written to ``.credentials.json`` with ownership ``1000:1000``
and mode ``0600``.

get.summary: Deploy server's own chat-agent skill description
get.description:
Return the Markdown skill description for the deploy server itself.

This lets the deploy server register as a virtual component with
``chat_base_url`` pointing at itself, and the roster endpoint's
probe succeeds against this handler.

schema.title: Response Deploy Chat Skill Chat Skill Get

get.summary: List components reachable by the chat agent
get.description:
Return a roster of components the chat agent can interact with.

Each entry has ``id``, ``base_url``, and ``skill`` (the Markdown body
fetched live from the component's ``GET /chat-skill``).  Components
that do not have ``allow_chat_access`` enabled are omitted.  A
component whose skill probe fails is served from its last-known-good
cached skill when one exists (stale-while-error), so a transient
probe failure does not drop it from the roster; it is omitted only
when it has never been probed successfully.

Skill bodies are cached for 60 seconds; a component whose cached
entry has expired is re-probed on the next request.

schema.title: Response List Chat Components Chat Components Get

put.summary: Update non-secret config keys for an allowlisted service
put.description:
Update non-secret config keys for an allowlisted service.

Saves the current config as a rollback snapshot before applying the
update.  Secret keys are rejected with 403.  Writes the merged config
to the service's config volume and records an audit entry for every
changed key.

403.description: Service not allowlisted or secret key rejected

post.summary: Restore the previous config version for an allowlisted service
post.description:
Restore the previous config snapshot for an allowlisted service.

Returns 404 when no previous snapshot exists (e.g. no config update
has been performed yet through the chat surface).

Restart an allowlisted service. Idempotent.

Raises 403 if the service is not in the chat-agent allowlist.
Rate-limited to one restart per 60 seconds per service.

Pull the latest image and recreate the container for an allowlisted service.

Synchronous — waits for the deploy to complete before returning.
Rate-limited to one update per 300 seconds per service.

get.summary: Read recent chat-agent mutation audit entries
get.description:
Return recent chat-agent audit entries, most-recent-first.

Optionally filter by *component* name.

get.summary: List recent GitHub Actions workflow runs for a repository
get.description:
List *owner*/*repo*'s workflow runs, most-recent-first.

*branch* and *run_status* (GitHub's ``status`` query param — e.g.
``"in_progress"``, ``"completed"``, ``"queued"``) narrow the results.
*per_page* is capped at 100 (GitHub's own page-size ceiling).

schema.title: Response List Workflow Runs Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Actions Runs Get

404.description: Repository not found or App not installed on it

get.summary: Get a single GitHub Actions workflow run
get.description: Get *owner*/*repo*'s workflow run *run_id* (status, conclusion, URL).

schema.title: Response Get Workflow Run Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Actions Runs  Run Id  Get

get.description: Get *owner*/*repo*'s details (visibility, description, settings).

schema.title: Response Get Repo Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Get

404.description: Repository not found or App not installed on it

patch.description: Update *owner*/*repo*'s settings — only provided fields are changed.

schema.title: Response Update Repo Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Patch

404.description: Repository not found or App not installed on it

422.description: No fields provided, or GitHub rejected the request

get.summary: List pull requests for a repository
get.description:
List *owner*/*repo*'s pull requests, most-recently-updated first.

*state* is one of ``"open"`` (default), ``"closed"``, or ``"all"``.
*per_page* is capped at 100 (GitHub's own page-size ceiling).

schema.title: Response List Pulls Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Pulls Get

404.description: Repository not found or App not installed on it

get.description: Get *owner*/*repo*'s pull request *number* (status, mergeable, URL).

schema.title: Response Get Pull Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Pulls  Number  Get

post.description:
Merge *owner*/*repo*'s pull request *pull_number*.

The optional *merge_method* (``merge``, ``squash``, ``rebase``) and
*sha* (expected HEAD SHA guard) are passed through to GitHub.  When
the repository uses a merge queue the endpoint enqueues the PR
rather than merging directly — the response ``merged`` field is
``true`` when the enqueue succeeded.

Requires a write-capable credential: the GitHub App installation
token (same as repo-update/security-features). Returns 503 if the
App is not configured, 404 if the App is not installed on the repo,
405 if the merge is not allowed (e.g. required status checks
failing or merge-queue blocking), 409 on merge conflicts, and 422
for other GitHub-side rejections.

schema.title: Response Merge Pull Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Pulls  Pull Number  Merge Post

404.description: PR or repository not found, or App not installed on it

405.description: Merge not allowed (branch protection, merge-queue required, or the PR is not in a mergeable state)

502.description: GitHub API returned an unexpected error

get.summary: Get default workflow permissions for a repository
get.description:
Get *owner*/*repo*'s default workflow permissions and whether Actions
can create and approve pull requests.

schema.title: Response Get Workflow Permissions Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Actions Permissions Workflow Get

404.description: Repository not found or App not installed on it

put.summary: Set default workflow permissions for a repository
put.description:
Set *owner*/*repo*'s default workflow permissions and whether Actions
can create and approve pull requests.

schema.title: Response Set Workflow Permissions Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Actions Permissions Workflow Put

404.description: Repository not found or App not installed on it

put.summary: Enable the Dependency graph / Dependabot vulnerability alerts
put.description:
Enable *owner*/*repo*'s Dependency graph and Dependabot alerts.

Without this, GitHub Actions checks that rely on the dependency graph
(e.g. ``dependency-review-action``) fail with "Dependency review is not
supported on this repository" until a human visits Settings > Security.
New repos get this automatically via :func:`create_repo`; this endpoint
covers repos that predate that, or ones created outside the chat agent.

schema.title: Response Enable Vulnerability Alerts Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Vulnerability Alerts Put

404.description: Repository not found or App not installed on it

put.description:
Enable or disable security features on *owner*/*repo*.

This combines what would otherwise require two separate GitHub API
calls (``vulnerability-alerts`` and ``automated-security-fixes``)
into one endpoint.  Only the features whose body fields are provided
are changed; omit a field to leave it as-is.

schema.title: Response Set Security Features Chat Github Repos  Owner   Repo  Security Features Put

404.description: Repository not found or App not installed on it

422.description: No fields provided, or GitHub rejected the request

503.description: Neither GitHub App nor PAT configured for this operation

post.description:
Create a new repository under the configured account.

Uses ``github_repo_create_token`` (a PAT), not the GitHub App
installation token — GitHub Apps cannot create repositories under a
personal account. The repository is always created under that token's
own account; there is no way to target an arbitrary owner.

Also enables the Dependency graph / Dependabot alerts (see
:func:`enable_vulnerability_alerts`) so CI checks that depend on it
(e.g. ``dependency-review-action``) don't fail on day one.

schema.title: Response Create Repo Chat Github Repos Post

422.description: GitHub rejected the request (e.g. invalid name)

post.summary: Deploy a repo+branch into the preview slot
post.description:
Deploy *repo_url* at *branch* into the single reusable preview slot.

Clones the repo, builds the Docker image via compose when needed,
creates + starts the container on the proxy network, and registers
the preview component so the gateway can route to it.

A new deploy replaces whatever currently occupies the slot.

400.description: Bad request — missing compose file or clone failure

post.description: Tear down the preview slot — stop/remove the container and clean up.

get.description:
Return the Langfuse project aliases whose key pairs are configured.

Only projects with both a public key and a secret key set are listed.

schema.title: Response List Projects Chat Langfuse Projects Get

get.summary: List Langfuse traces for a project
get.description:
Proxy ``GET /api/public/traces`` to Langfuse.

Common query parameters (``limit``, ``page``, ``tags``, ``name``,
``sessionId``, ``fromTimestamp``) are forwarded.  ``limit`` is capped
at 100 server-side.
Proxy ``GET /api/public/traces/{trace_id}`` to Langfuse.

404.description: Unknown project alias or trace not found

get.summary: List Langfuse observations for a project
get.description:
Proxy ``GET /api/public/observations`` to Langfuse.

Common query parameters (``limit``, ``page``, ``name``, ``type``,
``traceId``, ``fromStartTime``) are forwarded.  ``limit`` is capped
at 100 server-side.
Proxy ``GET /api/public/observations/{observation_id}`` to Langfuse.

404.description: Unknown project alias or observation not found

get.description: Handle requests to ``/`` — only active for subdomain-routed components.

put.description: Handle requests to ``/`` — only active for subdomain-routed components.

post.description: Handle requests to ``/`` — only active for subdomain-routed components.

delete.description: Handle requests to ``/`` — only active for subdomain-routed components.

options.description: Handle requests to ``/`` — only active for subdomain-routed components.

head.description: Handle requests to ``/`` — only active for subdomain-routed components.

patch.description: Handle requests to ``/`` — only active for subdomain-routed components.

ActionResponse.title: ActionResponse
ActionResponse.description: Generic response for start / stop / restart requests.

ActionType.title: ActionType
ActionType.description: Action type for start / stop / restart / rollback requests.

AuditFinding.title: AuditFinding
AuditFinding.description: A threshold-breach finding produced by a scan pass.

ChatAgentAuditEntryResponse.title: ChatAgentAuditEntryResponse
ChatAgentAuditEntryResponse.description: One audit-log entry exposed by GET /chat/audit-log.

ChatAgentConfigRollbackResponse.title: ChatAgentConfigRollbackResponse
ChatAgentConfigRollbackResponse.description: Response body for PUT /chat/config/{name} and POST /chat/config/{name}/rollback.

ChatAgentConfigUpdate.title: ChatAgentConfigUpdate
ChatAgentConfigUpdate.description:
Request body for PUT /chat/config/{name}.

Only non-secret keys are accepted; secret fields are rejected with 403.

ComponentSuggestItem.title: ComponentSuggestItem
ComponentSuggestItem.description: Lightweight component info for the config-form URL suggest feature.

ConfigImportResponse.title: ConfigImportResponse
ConfigImportResponse.description: Body of the 200 response from POST /services/{name}/config/import.

ConfigSchemaRefreshResponse.title: ConfigSchemaRefreshResponse
ConfigSchemaRefreshResponse.description: Body of the 200 response from POST /services/{name}/config/refresh-schema.

ContainerHealthSummary.title: ContainerHealthSummary
ContainerHealthSummary.description: Health snapshot for one sibling container.

ContractRefreshResponse.title: ContractRefreshResponse
ContractRefreshResponse.description: Body of the 200 response from POST /services/{name}/refresh-contract.

DeployAcceptedResponse.title: DeployAcceptedResponse
DeployAcceptedResponse.description: Returned by POST /services/{name}/deploy (202) when the job is queued.

DeployHistoryEntry.title: DeployHistoryEntry
DeployHistoryEntry.description:
One entry in a component's deploy-history ledger.

Recorded on every successful deploy (manual, caretaker, or rollback).

DeployJobPhase.title: DeployJobPhase
DeployJobPhase.description: Phases of a background deploy job.

DeployRequest.title: DeployRequest
DeployRequest.description: Optional image override for a deploy request.

DeploySource.title: DeploySource
DeploySource.description: Source of a deploy: manual operator action, caretaker auto-update, or rollback.

DerivedSpec.title: DerivedSpec
DerivedSpec.description: Parsed output from a service repo's docker-compose.yml.

DiskUsageResponse.title: DiskUsageResponse
DiskUsageResponse.description: Host disk usage + Docker storage breakdown.

DockerDfStats.title: DockerDfStats
DockerDfStats.description: Docker storage breakdown from ``docker system df``.

EnvSyncResponse.title: EnvSyncResponse
EnvSyncResponse.description: Body of the 200 response from POST /services/{name}/env/sync-keys.

merge_method.title: Merge Method
merge_method.description: Merge method: ``merge`` (default for most repos), ``squash``, or ``rebase``. Omit to use the repo/PR default.

sha.title: Sha
sha.description: Expected HEAD SHA of the pull request. When provided this is passed through to GitHub as a guard against merging a branch that has moved since the operator last reviewed it.

MergePullRequest.title: MergePullRequest
MergePullRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/merge``.

Both fields are optional; GitHub applies the repo/PR-appropriate
defaults when they are omitted.

OnboardConfirmAcceptedResponse.title: OnboardConfirmAcceptedResponse
OnboardConfirmAcceptedResponse.description: Returned by POST /onboard/confirm (202) when the job is queued.

OnboardJobPhase.title: OnboardJobPhase
OnboardJobPhase.description: Phases of a background onboard deploy job.

OrphanVolume.title: OrphanVolume
OrphanVolume.description: A Docker volume owned by no registered component and not in use.

ReclaimResponse.title: ReclaimResponse
ReclaimResponse.description: Result of a build-cache reclaim operation.

RollbackRequest.title: RollbackRequest
RollbackRequest.description:
Optional body for ``POST /services/{name}/rollback``.

When *digest* is absent/None the current one-step rollback behaviour
(swap deployed ↔ previous) is preserved.

dependency_graph.title: Dependency Graph
dependency_graph.description: Enable/disable the Dependency graph (shares the vulnerability-alerts endpoint with Dependabot alerts).

dependabot_alerts.title: Dependabot Alerts
dependabot_alerts.description: Enable/disable Dependabot vulnerability alerts (shares the vulnerability-alerts endpoint with the Dependency graph).

SecurityFeaturesRequest.title: SecurityFeaturesRequest
SecurityFeaturesRequest.description:
Body for ``PUT /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/security-features``.

All three fields are optional; only the ones provided are changed.
*dependency_graph* and *dependabot_alerts* share the same underlying
GitHub API (``vulnerability-alerts``), so setting either to ``true``
enables vulnerability alerts and setting either to ``false`` (without
the other being ``true``) disables them.  *dependabot_security_updates*
controls automated security fixes independently.

ServiceConfig.title: ServiceConfig
ServiceConfig.description: Persisted config for one non-primary (sibling) service in a multi-service component.

ServiceListResponse.title: ServiceListResponse
ServiceListResponse.description: Wrapper for the service list endpoint.

ServiceState.title: ServiceState
ServiceState.description: The seven lifecycle states a managed service can be in.

SiblingUpdateSummary.title: SiblingUpdateSummary
SiblingUpdateSummary.description: Per-sibling update-state snapshot so the UI can aggregate the group badge.

SystemSettingsResponse.title: SystemSettingsResponse
SystemSettingsResponse.description: Response model — inherits all fields from SystemSettings.

SystemSettingsUpdate.title: SystemSettingsUpdate
SystemSettingsUpdate.description: Update model — inherits all fields and validators from SystemSettings.

delete_branch_on_merge.title: Delete Branch On Merge
delete_branch_on_merge.description: Automatically delete head branches after merge.

UpdateRepoRequest.title: UpdateRepoRequest
UpdateRepoRequest.description:
Body for ``PATCH /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}``.

All fields are optional; only the ones provided are changed.
Unknown keys are rejected with 422.

UpdateState.title: UpdateState
UpdateState.description: Image-update availability derived from registry polling.

VolumeEntryType.title: VolumeEntryType
VolumeEntryType.description: Filesystem entry type returned by volume directory listing.

VolumeGrowthRecord.title: VolumeGrowthRecord
VolumeGrowthRecord.description: Growth record derived from comparing two consecutive snapshots.

VolumeStat.title: VolumeStat
VolumeStat.description: Size of a single Docker volume (from ``docker system df``).

can_approve_pull_request_reviews.title: Can Approve Pull Request Reviews
can_approve_pull_request_reviews.description: Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests.

WorkflowPermissionsRequest.title: WorkflowPermissionsRequest
WorkflowPermissionsRequest.description:
Body for ``PUT /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/permissions/workflow``.

Both fields are required by GitHub's API.
